steps.title: Identify the main subject
steps.content: The main subject is "Blaise Pascal's Pascaline device" — a singular noun phrase. The long em-dash interruption ("conceived... machine based methods") is a parenthetical and doesn't change the subject or verb.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
A singular subject in a historical context requires a singular past-tense finite verb.

A) being — a participle, not a finite verb; cannot serve as the main predicate. Wrong.
B) were — plural past; disagrees with the singular subject. Wrong.
C) was — singular past finite verb; agrees with "Pascaline device" and fits the historical past context. Correct.
D) are — plural present; disagrees with singular subject and uses present tense for a historical event. Wrong.

C is correct.
